warm-paper.$description: Warm Paper — Page canvas, card surfaces, and nav background — the base layer everything sits on

deep-ink.$description: Deep Ink — Primary text, body copy, nav links, and dominant border color — the structural voice

press-black.$description: Press Black — Heading text and heavy borders — slightly softer than pure black for large display sizes

charcoal-trace.$description: Charcoal Trace — Secondary borders, dividers, and muted text on dark surfaces

ash.$description: Ash — Helper text, disabled states, and hairline borders on light surfaces

dusty-mauve.$description: Dusty Mauve — Accent panel backgrounds, heading color, and chromatic border — a near-gray purple that signals brand moments without shouting

pale-sage.$description: Pale Sage — Secondary heading tint and accent border — a near-gray green that appears as quiet punctuation alongside the mauve

crimson-pro.$description: Display headlines at 44–50px, weight 300 with negative tracking. The anti-conventionally light serif whispers authority instead of projecting it; most agencies use 600–700 here, this restraint is the signature move.

azeret-mono.$description: Body copy, labels, and metadata at 12–18px. Monospace for a portfolio is a deliberate editorial choice — it reads as technical, precise, and un-decorative, contrasting the flowing serif headlines.

system-sans-serif.$description: Navigation links and micro-labels at 12px. Neutral utility voice that stays out of the way of the serif/mono dialogue.

warm-paper.$description: Surface level 1: Base page canvas and default card surface

ash-mist.$description: Surface level 2: Subtle raised surface tint for inset panels or muted containers

dusty-mauve-panel.$description: Surface level 3: Brand overlay panels (hero text block) — the only chromatic surface

charcoal-plane.$description: Surface level 4: Dark surface for inverted sections or image overlays
